title: maesa lanceolata subsp. lanceolata
intro: Maesa lanceolata subsp. lanceolata, also known as the African boxwood or yellow stinkwood, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive yellowish-brown bark and fragrant leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Maesa lanceolata subsp. lanceolata include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Various herbal preparations of Maesa lanceolata subsp. lanceolata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from skin irritations to respiratory infections.
